executionHistory.summary: Created spec.md, design.md, scopes.md with 8 scopes. Created report.md, uservalidation.md, state.json.

Reconcile-to-doc pass. Fixed: AnnotationHandlers wiring in main.go (was dead code), DeleteTag method+handler+route (missing), NATS event publishing in Store (missing), annotations config in smackerel.yaml (SST gap). 236 unit tests pass.
Audit phase: ./smackerel.sh check (config in sync, env_file drift guard OK), ./smackerel.sh lint (Go + Python clean, web manifests OK). Recorded in report.md Audit Evidence section.

Chaos phase: Honestly skipped — no spec-owned chaos harness exists for the annotation path. Annotations are deterministic CRUD with auth guard; failure modes (nil store, missing artifact, unauthorized) are covered by unit tests. Documented in report.md Chaos Evidence section.

Manual cross-check: spec.md/design.md/scopes.md aligned with implementation. All 8 scopes Done with verified file:line evidence. Annotation lifecycle (POST tag/note/highlight → store → NATS event → search/intelligence enrichment) matches spec intent.

Iter 10 trace-guard inventory only (no fixes applied). MIT-027-TRACE-001: Close 87 trace-guard failures via DoD-trace-prefix + report.md evidence references + Test Plan row additions + path corrections. Owner: bubbles.implement (parent-expanded bugfix-fastlane future iteration). Trigger: Next bubbles.goal session OR dedicated trace-cleanup sprint. Estimated effort: large (~6-8h, biggest residual). Failure breakdown: Type A (Gherkin scenario without faithful DoD): 33 — broadly distributed across Scopes 5/6/7/8 (annotation parsing, Telegram handler, mapping, search filters, intelligence integration); Type C (path-missing): 5 — scattered scopes referencing test files that need path correction; Type D (report missing evidence reference): 31 — internal/telegram/annotation_test.go (×11 Scope 6), internal/api/search_annotation_test.go (×10 Scope 7), internal/telegram/mapping_test.go (×4 Scope 5), internal/intelligence/annotations_test.go (×3 Scope 8), internal/annotation/parser_test.go (×3 Scope 2); Type E (no traceable Test Plan row): 17 — Scope 8 intelligence integration has 6 unmapped Gherkin scenarios (tag/rating/note relevance effects) plus Scope 5/6/7 scattered. Root cause: same as MIT-026-TRACE-001 — spec completed pre-traceability-guard era. Implementation tests EXIST (verified per spec-review entry above: ls confirms internal/annotation/, internal/telegram/, internal/api/, internal/intelligence/ all populated with annotation*.go and *_test.go files). The matrix between Gherkin scenarios → Test Plan rows → DoD bullets → report.md evidence references was never populated to G068 standards. Closure strategy: (1) Add `Scenario <ID or name>:` prefix to 33 DoD bullets; (2) Add 17 missing Test Plan rows pointing to existing test files in internal/intelligence/, internal/telegram/, internal/api/; (3) Bulk-add 31 evidence references in report.md citing the 5 test files listed above with their actual function lists; (4) Repoint 5 paths to existing test files. Sample fix: scopes.md Scope 8 DoD '`intelligence/annotations.go` adjusts relevance scores' → 'Scenarios "Tag annotation has small positive relevance effect" + "Rating annotation adjusts relevance score upward" + "Note annotation has small positive relevance effect" + "Low rating annotation adjusts relevance score downward" + "Interaction annotation boosts relevance" + "Relevance score does not go below 0": `intelligence/annotations.go` adjusts relevance scores per annotation type with floor at 0'; report.md Scope 6 evidence section: append 'Telegram annotation handler tests at `internal/telegram/annotation_test.go` cover SCN-027-* including TestHandleAnnotateCommand_*, TestParseAnnotationCommand_*, TestRouteAnnotationToHandler_*'. Status / certification fields untouched.

MIT-027-TRACE-001 closed. Trace-guard 87 → 0 failures; artifact-lint passes (only pre-existing scopeProgress/scopeLayout schema-deprecation warnings remain). Type A (33 DoD-trace prefixes): added 'Scenario "<name>": ' prefix to existing DoD bullets in scopes 1×1 (annotation types created by migration), 2×5 (full annotation, out-of-range rating, case-insensitive interaction, all interaction types, 'out of 5' syntax — three scenarios prefixed onto two DoD bullets that already covered them), 3×5 (NATS payload, CreateFromParsed rejects non-existent artifact, GetSummary aggregated + GetSummary error, tag add+remove), 4×5 (invalid rating + empty body share one bullet, GET summary + GET summary unannotated share one bullet, non-existent artifact), 5×2 (resolve from replied-to, multiple mappings same chat), 6×2 (plain text becomes note, /rate no args), 7×7 (tag filter, intent detection ×3 share one bullet, results include annotation data, boost adjusts ranking + boost small enough share one bullet), 8×6 (rating up/down/interaction/tag/note share one bullet, relevance does not go below 0). No DoD behavioral claim rewritten — prefixes prepended to existing text only. Type C (6 path repoints in Scope 1, not 5 as originally diagnosed — fuzzy row-matching also picked T1-06 'Migration applies cleanly' whose path internal/db/migrations_test.go was identically broken): all six T1-* rows repointed from internal/db/migrations_test.go (does not exist) → tests/integration/db_migration_test.go (real, contains TestMigrations_AllTablesExist, TestMigrations_AnnotationsConstraints, TestMigrations_IndexesExist, TestMigrations_TableDropAndRecreate, TestMigrations_SchemaVersionCount, TestMigrations_ExtensionsLoaded). Type D (report evidence): added Trace-Guard Closure section to report.md citing internal/annotation/parser_test.go, internal/telegram/mapping_test.go, internal/telegram/annotation_test.go, internal/api/search_annotation_test.go, internal/intelligence/annotations_test.go, and tests/integration/db_migration_test.go by full repo path with real test function lists. Type E (17 new Test Plan rows): T2-13..T2-20 (parser_test.go: 8 rows for parse rating/tags/tag-removal/interaction/note/out-of-range/all-interaction-types/out-of-5), T3-11 (store_test.go: CreateFromParsed converts), T4-13 (annotations_test.go: GET history), T5-06 (mapping_test.go: resolve from replied-to), T8-12..T8-17 (annotations_test.go: 6 rows for rating-up/rating-down/interaction/tag/note/no-below-0). Verification: traceability-guard 027 PASSED (70 scenarios, 103 test rows, 70 mapped to DoD, 0 unmapped); artifact-lint 027 PASSED (only pre-existing schema warnings). State.json: appended this executionHistory entry; status / certification fields untouched. No source code, test files, or production tests modified. Files modified: specs/027-user-annotations/{scopes.md, report.md, state.json}.

executionHistory.scope: feature-wide cross-spec closure (actor-source segment of MIT-027-TRACE-001)
executionHistory.summary: Cross-spec closure of the actor-source segment of MIT-027-TRACE-001 (the trust-boundary segment — annotation entry-points accept actor identity from request body in production with no claim-binding to authenticated session). The spec-027 trace-guard segment of MIT-027-TRACE-001 was already CLOSED on 2026-05-09 in the entry above; this entry closes the SECURITY actor-source segment that was carry-forward routed to spec 044. Closure ships in spec 044 Scope 02 (Hot-Path Middleware Integration + MIT Closures). VERDICT: completed_owned. What landed in spec 044 Scope 02: (1) `internal/api/annotations.go` — added `Environment string` field to AnnotationHandlers; refactored `CreateAnnotation` handler — reads body once into bytes via http.MaxBytesReader + io.ReadAll; in production, scans the body bytes for the literal JSON keys `"actor_source"` or `"actor_id"` and rejects with HTTP 400 (`actor_source in request body is forbidden in production` / `actor_id in request body is forbidden in production`) BEFORE any annotation store call; in dev/test the body keys are permitted (legacy ergonomic preserved). When a session is present, the session UserID is logged at annotation creation time. (2) `cmd/core/wiring.go` — AnnotationHandlers construction sets `Environment: cfg.Environment`. (3) Adversarial integration tests `tests/integration/auth_annotation_test.go` (NEW, build tag integration) — TestAnnotation_BodyActorSourceInProduction_Rejected (smuggle `actor_source` in body, asserts 400 + 'actor_source in request body is forbidden in production' AND that the stub annotation store's CreateFromParsed counter remains zero proving the rejection happens BEFORE any persistence); TestAnnotation_BodyActorIDInProduction_Rejected (mirror for `actor_id`); ALL PASS against the live test stack. Note: this closure addresses the API-surface defensive rejection. The full claim-binding contract (annotation pipeline derives actor_source from session for ALL entry points including Telegram bridge and NATS) remains a Scope 03 deliverable in spec 044 — Scope 02 lands the production-mode defensive rejection on the API entry path and the Environment field plumbing. The actor_source database column itself is unchanged in this scope; NO schema migration ships in spec 044 Scope 02 per the design.md §6.4 minimum-surface contract. Verification: ./smackerel.sh check EXIT=0; ./smackerel.sh test unit EXIT=0 (Go internal/api 9.520s ok); go vet -tags integration ./tests/integration/... EXIT=0; integration suite for the 2 new auth_annotation tests PASSES end-to-end against the live test stack. closedAt: 2026-05-10. closedBy: bubbles.implement (mode: spec 044 Scope 02 implement). closureSpec: 044-per-user-bearer-auth. closureSegment: actor-source-defensive-rejection. closureCommit: implement(044): Scope 02 — bearer auth middleware + MIT-040-S-008 + MIT-038-S-003 + MIT-027-TRACE-001 closures. closureEvidence: specs/044-per-user-bearer-auth/report.md#scope-02-implement-evidence. nextRequiredOwner=null (until spec 044 Scope 03 wires Telegram and NATS entry points). MIT-027-TRACE-001 actor-source segment CLOSED.
attestations: implement: AnnotationHandlers.CreateAnnotation production path now defensively rejects body actor_source AND actor_id smuggling BEFORE any store call; Environment field plumbed via cmd/core/wiring.go; session UserID logged at creation when present.

test: 2 adversarial integration tests in tests/integration/auth_annotation_test.go all PASS against the live test stack; the stub store's createCalls counter remains zero in both rejection cases proving the rejection precedes persistence.

validate: each adversarial test designed so removing the corresponding production rejection branch makes it fail; the tests construct full Dependencies with Environment='production' AND AuthConfig.Enabled=true. ./smackerel.sh check + go vet + go vet -tags integration all clean.

audit: spec 027 status / certification.* / scopeProgress / completedScopes / certifiedCompletedPhases ALL UNTOUCHED — spec 027 stays at done; only executionHistory appended with this closure entry; lastUpdatedAt bumped.

docs: closure narrative captured in specs/044-per-user-bearer-auth/report.md → Scope 02 Implement Evidence section; this state.json entry references that location and explicitly notes that Telegram + NATS entry-point claim-binding remains a spec 044 Scope 03 deliverable.

executionHistory.scope: feature-wide cross-spec closure (Telegram end-to-end coverage segment of MIT-027-TRACE-001)
executionHistory.summary: Cross-spec closure of the Telegram-end-to-end-coverage segment of MIT-027-TRACE-001 via spec 044 Scope 03. The actor-source defensive rejection at the API entry path was already CLOSED on 2026-05-10 (entry above) by spec 044 Scope 02, but at that time the supplementary end-to-end coverage proving the body-claimed-actor rejection ALSO works through the Telegram bridge entry point was deferred. Spec 044 Scope 03 lands `tests/integration/auth_telegram_e2e_test.go::TestTelegramBridge_BodyClaimedActorRejected` which mints a Telegram-issued PASETO via PerUserTokenMinter.MintForChat, sends a body with `actor_source: "telegram"` smuggled in, and asserts the production AnnotationHandlers.CreateAnnotation handler rejects it with HTTP 400. This proves the Scope 02 actor-source rejection works through the Telegram path end-to-end. Note: The remaining NATS-bus-segment closure for MIT-027-TRACE-001 (annotation pipeline derives actor_source from session for ALL entry points including raw NATS subjects) is NOT yet shipped — it remains a spec 044 Scope 04 deliverable per design.md §16.3 and is tracked by the F02 deferred-finalize-blocker (`PerUserTokenMinter` per-call wiring into the bot's outbound HTTP). closedAt: 2026-05-11. closedBy: bubbles.docs (mode: spec 044 Scope 03 docs publish). closureSpec: 044-per-user-bearer-auth. closureSegment: telegram-end-to-end-coverage. closureEvidence: specs/044-per-user-bearer-auth/report.md → Test Evidence (Scope 03) → Telegram Bridge subsection. nextRequiredOwner=null for the Telegram segment (NATS segment remains owned by spec 044 Scope 04). Spec 027 status / certification.* / scopeProgress / completedScopes / certifiedCompletedPhases UNTOUCHED — spec 027 stays at done; only executionHistory appended with this closure entry.
attestations: test: tests/integration/auth_telegram_e2e_test.go::TestTelegramBridge_BodyClaimedActorRejected PASSES against the live test stack — Telegram-minted PASETO admits middleware but body-claimed actor_source is rejected by the production AnnotationHandlers handler with HTTP 400.
audit: spec 027 status remains 'done'; certification.completedScopes / certification.certifiedCompletedPhases / scopeProgress UNCHANGED — only executionHistory appended.
docs: cross-reference recorded in this state.json entry; closure narrative lives in specs/044-per-user-bearer-auth/report.md Test Evidence (Scope 03).
